does: This paper introduces MUSE, a comprehensive evaluation benchmark for machine unlearning in language models that systematically measures six distinct properties of unlearned models. The benchmark assesses whether models successfully remove verbatim memorization and knowledge of target data, prevent privacy leakage through membership inference attacks, maintain utility on retained data, scale efficiently to large removal requests, and sustain performance across sequential unlearning operations. By defining concrete metrics for each dimension, MUSE provides a structured framework that goes beyond single-metric evaluation, enabling rigorous comparison of different approximate unlearning algorithms and their practical trade-offs.
connects: This work addresses limitations in prior machine unlearning research, which has largely evaluated algorithms through narrow lenses focusing on individual aspects like memorization removal or model utility. It builds on foundational unlearning papers that introduced approximate alternatives to full retraining, and extends recent work highlighting tensions between privacy, utility, and computational efficiency. The benchmark also incorporates insights from machine learning privacy literature, particularly membership inference attacks and data privacy evaluation, while responding to growing concerns about copyright and personal data in large language model training datasets. MUSE synthesizes these disparate evaluation approaches into a unified framework.
enables: This evaluation framework enables future work in machine unlearning by establishing clear benchmarking standards that can drive algorithm development toward practical deployment. Researchers can now identify which unlearning methods excel at specific properties while revealing trade-offs between them, motivating new algorithms that balance competing demands. The benchmark supports real-world deployment scenarios by providing evidence of scalability and sequential robustness, critical for production systems handling multiple data removal requests. Additionally, MUSE could facilitate regulatory compliance assessment, help organizations understand their obligations under data deletion laws, and establish baseline standards that might inform future guidelines for responsible language model development and deployment.
